## 1. Role Justification

The Gameplay Engineer #2 is a mid-level role focused on UI scripting, animation integration, bug fixing, and automated testing. This is a P1 hire required for Stage 5 (Full Production). The role sits under Dmitri Volkov's direction, complementing Gameplay Engineer #1 (G16) with a stronger focus on UI scripting and animation rather than core gameplay mechanics.

This hire provides additional implementation capacity for UI/animation features while maintaining code quality through automated testing practices.

**Pipeline Stages Owned:** 3 (UI Scripting/Animation Implementation), 5 (Feature Development), 6 (Code Review Participation)

# Position Specification Document

**Role:** Gameplay Engineer #2 (G17)
**Role Family:** Engineering — Gameplay
**Seniority:** Mid-Level (L1)
**Team:** Casual Games Studio — Engineering Division
**Reports To:** Dmitri Volkov (Senior Game Engineer)
**Priority:** P1
**Compensation Band:** $90K–$120K base

---

## 1. Role Justification

The Gameplay Engineer #2 is a mid-level role focused on UI scripting, animation integration, bug fixing, and automated testing. This is a P1 hire required for Stage 5 (Full Production). The role sits under Dmitri Volkov's direction, complementing Gameplay Engineer #1 (G16) with a stronger focus on UI scripting and animation rather than core gameplay mechanics.

This hire provides additional implementation capacity for UI/animation features while maintaining code quality through automated testing practices.

**Pipeline Stages Owned:** 3 (UI Scripting/Animation Implementation), 5 (Feature Development), 6 (Code Review Participation)

---

## 2. Required Competencies (weighted)

| Competency              | Weight | Minimum Threshold | Assessment Method         |
| ----------------------- | ------ | ----------------- | ------------------------- |
| UI Scripting (C#/Unity) | 30%    | ≥ 3.5/5           | UI coding challenge       |
| Animation Integration   | 25%    | ≥ 3.5/5           | Animation system exercise |
| Bug Fixing & Debugging  | 20%    | ≥ 3.5/5           | Debugging challenge       |
| Automated Testing       | 15%    | ≥ 3/5             | Test writing exercise     |
| Team Collaboration      | 10%    | ≥ 3/5             | Behavioral interview      |

---

## 3. Must-Have Qualifications

- 2+ years gameplay programming with focus on UI/animation
- C# UI framework development; event-driven UI architecture
- Spine 2D or equivalent animation system integration
- Bug fixing and testing practices with measurable impact
- Attention to detail with high bug fix acceptance rate

---

## 4. Compensation Structure

| Component         | Range / Value                 |
| ----------------- | ----------------------------- |
| Base Salary       | $90K–$120K (target: $105K)    |
| Performance Bonus | Up to 8% of base (target: 6%) |
| Equity            | 0.04%–0.08% (Mid-Level tier)  |
| Signing Bonus     | Up to $8K                     |
| Relocation        | Up to $5K                     |
| Conference Budget | $3K/year                      |

---

## 5. Success Criteria (30/60/90 Day Plan)

| Milestone   | Criteria                                                                       |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Day 1**   | Engine access granted, dev environment running, repo access configured         |
| **Week 1**  | First PR merged (UI bug fix); introduced to Dmitri Volkov and team             |
| **Month 1** | First UI feature implemented; animation integration complete for first feature |
| **Month 2** | Automated UI test suite established; bug fix acceptance rate > 95%             |
| **Month 3** | Active contributor to UI codebase; maintains automated UI tests                |

---
